scenarios.name: Primary entity records expose the next-build minimum canonical fields
Lead and job posting follow forward-only lifecycle progression
Posting-contact links are stored as one canonical relationship row per pair
Contact and posting-contact states advance with the workflow
Contact identity may be reused across multiple postings without duplicating the person record
Source metadata is owned by the lead rather than the posting
Valid non-ambiguous JD creates a posting linked back to the lead
Missing or invalid JD does not auto-create a posting
Identifiable poster profile creates lead-contact traceability
Founder is treated as a first-class inferred recipient type
New postings merge only when identity matching is confident
Operational history is retained instead of broadly hard-deleted by default
Raw text input takes precedence over URLs during LinkedIn Scraping normalization
Consolidated runtime secrets can materialize vendor-specific local secret files
Provider integrations use normalized internal handling rather than vendor-specific orchestration logic
External integration failures surface as normalized blocked or failed reasons
External source material used by the workflow is persisted internally for later review
Manual browser capture creates a capture bundle and canonical raw source
Manual capture defaults selected-text and full-page flows differently before converging into one bundle contract
Selected text from manual capture is preserved verbatim
Autonomous Gmail job-alert intake persists the alert snapshot and JD-fetch provenance
Autonomous Gmail parser prefers the plain-text digest and can emit multiple parsed job cards
Autonomous lead creation can proceed from a LinkedIn guest JD even when the exact company-hosted page is not recovered
Autonomous JD is persisted fully in markdown before structured extraction
Autonomous posting files are grouped under a company or role workspace
Component-oriented layout keeps runtime artifacts under the owning component folders
Paste inbox ingestion creates one canonical lead raw source artifact
Explicit new paste source replaces the live canonical lead source while later normalized-only reruns preserve it
Derived lead files are split from the canonical raw source with review metadata
Well-structured pasted lead dumps succeed with the rule-based first pass alone
Recruiter-authored pasted lead dump with plain-language hiring text is recognized by the rule-based first pass
Networking-relevant post hints are preserved while obvious job-CTA chrome is dropped
Recruiter-profile chrome is cleaned from the derived poster profile in the Wilcore-style lead format
Ambiguous source dumps are escalated for review when no second-pass classifier is configured
Ambiguous lead review still publishes a blocked manifest
Optional AI second pass can replace an ambiguous rule split only when confidence improves
scenarios.note: The PRD keeps the AI second pass optional for the current build, so the bounded rule-based split path is still the implemented default.

scenarios.name: Vendor usage is auditable without exposing secrets
Gmail integration remains a channel rather than the canonical system of record
A stage is not successful until both state and required artifacts are persisted
Canonical-state write failure marks the stage failed
Required handoff artifact publication failure marks the stage failed
Retry occurs at the smallest safe unit of work
Draft-generation retry exhaustion surfaces review instead of silent skip
Ambiguous send state prefers under-sending to duplicate-sending
Feedback delay is not treated as pipeline failure
Feedback ingestion is idempotent for the same logical message outcome
Hard-ineligible lead is stopped before tailoring
Current hard experience threshold is one global rule rather than role-family-specific policy
Missing eligibility signals are treated as unknown instead of hard failure
Eligibility evaluation persists an audit artifact with evidence
Tailoring workspace is created with required artifacts
Workspace metadata captures the current tailoring constraints
Scope guard prevents edits outside the allowed tailoring boundary
Base resume track is selected automatically and persisted in workspace metadata
Tailoring run lifecycle is persisted from bootstrap through mandatory agent review
Current default editing scope stays narrow unless metadata expands it
Tailored resume shows credible overlap with the target role and JD intent
JD-relevant stack is reflected in summary-adjacent sections, work experience, and skills ordering
Step artifacts preserve JD-only extraction and honest evidence mapping
JD signal weighting uses one current global default scale
Step 6 payload uses the current structured edit contract
Step 5 controlled elaboration stays within allowed bounded evidence moves
Work-experience bullets preserve numeric metrics and resume-writing discipline
Final output remains compile-safe, one-page, and visually disciplined
Finalize requires valid intelligence and one-page output
Insufficient evidence or scope conflicts return explicit revision guidance instead of guessing
Tailoring overrides are persisted when the owner changes an agent-reviewed tailoring decision
Role-targeted flow runs a mandatory agent review without a mandatory human pause
Review rejection followed by retailoring preserves run history
Role-targeted contact search starts with Apollo people search
Apollo people search resolves company identity before people search and tolerates sparse candidate rows
Initial enrichment stays selective and capped to the current shortlist policy
LinkedIn URL from enrichment can produce a recipient profile snapshot before drafting
Shortlisted candidate that becomes a terminal enrichment dead end is removed from canonical shortlist state
Person-scoped email discovery uses the current provider order and stops on first usable result
Discovery returns either a usable email or an explicit unresolved outcome
Provider-specific no-match responses normalize to explicit internal not-found outcomes
Discovery reuses known working email only when identity is clear
Current build does not reuse one contact's pattern for other contacts at the same company
Discovery resolves company domain internally and records a distinct domain-unresolved outcome when needed
Discovery updates provider-budget state and falls back when a provider is exhausted
Discovery confidence follows current pre-send and post-send policy
Discovery retry after a bounced result skips the provider that already failed
Provider exhaustion for a contact surfaces review instead of endless retries
Discovery history is preserved as cascade-level attempts without overwriting prior outcomes
Unresolved and bounced discovery review surfaces remain queryable
Role-targeted outreach uses posting, recipient, and tailored resume context
General learning outreach does not require a tailored resume or posting linkage
Repeat outreach requiring interpretation is not auto-sent
Autonomous role-targeted sending respects company-level pacing
Current autonomous send set prefers recruiter, manager-adjacent, and engineer coverage without a global daily cap
Role-targeted one-step outreach uses personalization, overlap, and a low-friction ask
Drafting begins only after the full current send set is ready
The active ready send set is drafted before automatic sending begins
Failed drafts do not block sending for successfully drafted contacts in the same ready set
Recruiter and team-adjacent outreach defaults to a short Zoom conversation ask
Rich HTML outreach preserves readable fallback content and canonical message persistence
Human-readable and machine-readable drafting artifacts are both available
Default v4 shared template uses the current unified structure
Internal-helper or alumni outreach may include a forwardable summary snippet
Delivery feedback uses mailbox observation with the current timing rules
Delivery feedback may begin immediately for each sent message
Delivery feedback persists canonical event history and machine handoff output
Bounced outcomes do not automatically rewrite discovery cache in the build
Delayed feedback scheduling uses launchd in the current deployment
scenarios.note: `job_hunt_copilot.local_runtime`, `scripts/ops/run_feedback_sync.py`, `scripts/ops/materialize_feedback_sync_plist.py`, and the new `ops/launchd/job-hunt-copilot-feedback-sync.plist` now give delayed feedback polling a dedicated launchd invocation path that still calls the shared Delivery Feedback sync logic.

scenarios.name: Not-bounced is recorded when the observation window closes without a bounce
Reply detection retains useful reply context without requiring reply classification
Bounce and not-bounced outcomes are reusable for discovery while replies remain review-only
Feedback events are tied to the exact sent message instance
Feedback events are persisted immediately when detected
Reply outcomes remain reviewable without entering the current discovery-learning loop

rules.note: The components exist and now have committed smoke coverage across tailoring, discovery, send, delayed feedback, and review-query boundaries, but the autonomous supervisor still does not orchestrate the full downstream pipeline past lead handoff.

scenarios.name: End-to-end retry resumes from the last successful stage boundary
scenarios.note: Focused supervisor regressions now prove downstream retries keep the same `pipeline_run_id`, blocked stage, and pending review packet instead of restarting from `lead_handoff`, but the later-stage autonomous actions are still not registered.

scenarios.note: The feature file explicitly marks two-step outreach as outside the required acceptance surface for the current build.

rules.rule: Current-build safety, privacy, and evidence-grounding boundaries

scenarios.note: Explicit BA-10 hardening regressions now verify unsupported tailoring asks stay as gaps, automatic outreach still requires approved review and blocks repeat ambiguity, and review-oriented outputs stay summary-level.

gap_registry.title: Integrated smoke harness is still missing
gap_registry.reason: The repo has strong component tests, but no committed cross-component smoke run that exercises bootstrap through review-query surfaces in one pass.
gap_registry.evidence_summary: The committed smoke harness now exercises bootstrap through review-query reads in one deterministic cross-component flow.

gap_registry.title: Supervisor orchestration still stops at lead handoff
gap_registry.reason: The durable heartbeat, selector ordering, and retry-safe run persistence exist, but the registered action catalog still only advances autonomous work through `lead_handoff`; later stages reselect the same durable run and escalate instead of executing.
gap_registry.evidence_summary: Selector ordering, durable run reuse, and unsupported-stage escalation are covered, but later-stage autonomous actions remain unregistered.
scenario_names: Supervisor work selection follows the current default priority order
Role-targeted orchestration follows the current dependency order
General learning outreach bypasses the role-targeted agent-review requirement
Role-targeted flow completes from LinkedIn Scraping through delivery feedback
End-to-end retry resumes from the last successful stage boundary

gap_registry.title: Maintenance workflow and artifacts are not implemented
gap_registry.reason: The schema and runtime pack reserve maintenance surfaces, but there is no autonomous maintenance batch workflow, no maintenance artifacts, and no maintenance review flow yet.
gap_registry.evidence_summary: Schema and runtime scaffolding reserve maintenance surfaces, but there is still no maintenance module, runner, or review-artifact workflow.
scenario_names: Maintenance change artifacts exist for every autonomous maintenance batch
Daily maintenance is mandatory, bounded, and run-boundary aware
Maintenance changes follow the current git and approval workflow
Proper maintenance validation requires both change-scoped and full-project testing
Failed or unapproved maintenance batches remain reviewable

gap_registry.title: Chat review and control remain wrapper-only
gap_registry.reason: The direct `jhc-chat` entrypoint manages chat session lifecycle, but richer review retrieval, control routing, and expert-guidance behaviors are not yet implemented in the chat surface.
gap_registry.evidence_summary: Chat lifecycle, review-query reads, and bootstrap scaffolding exist, but chat itself still does not retrieve grouped reviews or route control decisions.
scenario_names: jhc-chat startup dashboard is detailed, bounded, and clean-first
Startup dashboard runtime metrics count only active autonomous execution
Review retrieval is grouped, compact-first, and newest-first within each group
jhc-chat uses persisted state for answers and control routing
Default change summaries cover activity since the last completed expert review
Expert guidance becomes live immediately but conflicting or uncertain reuse asks first
Conflicting expert guidance pauses the whole autonomous system
Expert-requested background tasks require explicit handoff summary and exclusive focus
Expert-requested background task outcomes return to review appropriately
AI agent surfaces the current review queue in chat

gap_registry.title: Idle-timeout resume is still backlog
gap_registry.reason: Explicit-close and explicit-resume paths exist, but unexpected `jhc-chat` exits still require a later explicit resume because automatic idle-timeout recovery is not implemented.
gap_registry.evidence_summary: Unexpected chat exit is recorded and a later explicit resume works, but no automatic idle-timeout resume helper exists.
scenario_names: Expert-interaction resume follows explicit close, explicit resume, or safe idle timeout

gap_registry.title: Delayed feedback scheduling is not wired to a scheduler
gap_registry.reason: Delivery feedback syncing can run and persists scheduler metadata, but there is no committed launchd-driven delayed feedback poller yet.
gap_registry.evidence_summary: The dedicated delayed-feedback launchd runner, plist materialization, and smoke-covered sync path are now committed.

gap_registry.title: Sleep and wake recovery is not implemented beyond metadata
gap_registry.reason: Supervisor cycles record the intended sleep/wake detection method, but the actual pmset-log parsing and conservative fallback logic have not been implemented.
gap_registry.evidence_summary: Supervisor heartbeats now parse pmset sleep/wake evidence first and fall back to a conservative cycle-gap recovery heuristic.

gap_registry.title: Posting-abandon control surface is missing
gap_registry.reason: There is no explicit user-facing or runtime control path that abandons a posting from arbitrary active orchestration states while preserving canonical history.
gap_registry.evidence_summary: Agent-level start/stop/pause/resume/replan controls exist, but there is still no posting-scoped abandon command or runtime mutation path.
scenario_names: The user may explicitly abandon a posting from any active orchestration state
